confidence_rationale: Comprehensive review treating serotonin as a unified affective control signal across appetitive and aversive motivated action, with explicit treatment of asymmetries between reward and punishment statistics that explain why a tonic 5-HT floor is needed. The review does not use REE's three-stage language but provides the computational and empirical grounding for exactly that structure.
failure_signatures: Serotonin's role in aversive processing is only a partial mirror of dopamine's role in reward due to natural asymmetries in reward vs punishment statistics -- implying a tonic regulatory role cannot be straightforwardly derived from phasic dopamine analogies
The review notes that behavioural data in humans on serotonin's role remains inconsistent, limiting confident mapping from rodent/invertebrate computational models

mapping.source_claim_statement: Serotonin provides a computational view of affective control over appetitively and aversively motivated actions; its role in aversive processing is analogous to but asymmetric with dopamine's role in reward, due to natural statistics of punishments vs rewards.
mapping.ree_translation: The asymmetry argument directly supports INV-052's claim that a distinct tonic regulatory system is required: because punishment statistics are not simply the inverse of reward statistics, dopamine's phasic benefit-signalling cannot serve double-duty as the harm-environment regulator. A dedicated tonic 5-HT floor (MECH-186) is architecturally necessary, not merely convenient.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The review's computational framework is built around RL value functions and does not map directly onto REE's latent terrain architecture. The three-stage decomposition (terrain, transduction, maintenance) is not present in the source -- it is an REE-side inference from the review's unified treatment of affective control.
mapping.source_context: Review paper spanning invertebrate to mammalian data; primarily rodent electrophysiology and pharmacology, with human behavioural pharmacology sections.

confidence_components.notes: Annual Review of Neuroscience carries high prestige; Dayan and Huys are the canonical computational authorities on serotonin function. Mapping fidelity slightly reduced because the review does not distinguish the three INV-052 stages. Transfer risk from RL framework to REE latent architecture is moderate but manageable.
